summary: Excellent soundboard recording of the legendary 5/7/77 Boston show, highlighted by a scorching Half‑Step, soaring Eyes→Wheel→Wharf Rat and warm, balanced mix.
review: The Charlie Miller SBD capture delivers crystal‑clear, warm sound that lets every nuance of the May 7 1977 performance shine.  The band is in top form – Jerry’s Travis Bean guitar sings (even if a few hear it as a bit metallic), Keith’s keyboards sparkle on FOTD, and the dual drummers drive an unforgettable Eyes→Wheel→Wharf Rat sequence.  Standout numbers include Mississippi Half‑Step, Music Never Stopped, Bertha, Peggy‑O and Terrapin Station, making this one of the top‑tier shows of the famed May ’77 run.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 90 % of the reviews are 5‑star, praising both performance and soundboard quality; only a handful of lower scores mention minor guitar tone or tuning issues.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller’s SBD is warm, well‑balanced with crisp highs and solid low end; slight echo/hint of reverb noted but generally praised as superb. No major drop‑outs or hiss.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as "chock‑full" of Jerry bests; second set flows seamlessly with a classic Eyes→Wheel→Wharf Rat sequence and high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar tone repeatedly called "smoking", "fast‑paced" and "on fire"; a few note the Travis Bean’s metallic edge and occasional tuning wobble, but overall his playing is praised as among the best of ’77.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass intro on Eyes highlighted as “nice” and the rhythm section noted for tightness; Phil’s playing generally lauded.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's keyboard work singled out on FOTD and other jams, described as "beautiful" and "exactly perfect".
band_member_comments.Drums: Dual drummers (Bill & Mickey) receive high marks, especially on the Drum > Wheel segment; rhythm section called "bursting with energy".
